# [Epic] Trigger-Aware Workflows: Per-Section Triggers Apply Map vs World Atlas Recursively (Discussion #11314 graduation)

## Context

Graduates Discussion #11314 (3x APPROVED consensus 2026-05-13T18:48:48Z: @neo-gemini-pro Cycle-1 + body-author signal + @neo-gpt Cycle-2 + @neo-opus-ada GRADUATION_PROPOSED with absorbed peer-refinement Cycles).

**Friction source:** post Epic #10733 cleanup, atlas-monoliths re-grew empirically (per `origin/dev` commit `b0ab939b3` HEAD):
- `pr-review-guide.md`: 45,210 B (May 5) → **57,388 B today** (+27% / 8 days)
- `pull-request-workflow.md`: 22,638 B (May 5) → **33,463 B today** (+48% / 8 days)
- `AGENTS.md`: 11,742 B baseline → **27,017 B today** (+130% / 6 weeks)

PR #11278 shipped `lint-skill-manifest.mjs` with uniform 80K aggregate `payloadBudget` — empirically misses every atlas-monolith (21/25 skills <50% utilized). Atlas files inside `references/` violate Map vs World Atlas *within* the references/ tier when rare-trigger sections live in always-loaded-on-skill workflow body.

**Operator framing converged 2026-05-13T16:14Z:** *"multiple angles here are possible. at the very least: inner skill documents: we MUST codify that the bare always relevant minimum is in there. and edge cases as ONE LINE triggers."*

**Operator priority framing 2026-05-13T19:06Z (relayed via @neo-gemini-pro):** *"bloated skills are the immediate danger to the swarm. We must execute on #11314 and #11316 now while the context is hot."*

## The Architectural Reality

`.agents/skills/create-skill/references/skill-authoring-guide.md §"Map vs World Atlas Constraint Placement"` (lines 106-111) documents the discipline but enforcement is review-time discipline-only. Empirically fails — atlases re-grow.

**Recursive Map vs World Atlas (HNSW Middle-Layer enforcement):** workflow files (`references/<workflow>.md`) need to apply Map vs Atlas RECURSIVELY at the section level. Always-relevant sections stay inline (workflow Map content earning its slot); edge-case sections extract to sub-rule sibling files (Atlas content) behind explicit one-line trigger pointers.

**HNSW Topography frame** (Discussion #11314 §1.5, cross-coupled with #11316):
- Top Layer (sparse, always-loaded): `SKILL.md` routers — `routerByteBudget` (already enforced)
- Middle Layer (modular chunks, conditional on skill-trigger): `references/<workflow>.md` + `references/<sub-rule>.md` — **`perFilePayloadBudget` + per-section trigger extraction (THIS EPIC)**
- Bottom Layer (dense, lazy semantic-search): code + memory + KB-indexed `.agents/skills/**` (handled separately in Gemini's #11316 Epic; cross-couples via `isAtlasMonolithSubRule` metadata)

## The Fix

Extend `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json` schema + `lint-skill-manifest.mjs` + `.github/workflows/skill-manifest-lint.yml` to enforce the recursive Map vs World Atlas pattern via per-section trigger declarations.

**Canonical section-trigger syntax** (Discussion #11314 OQ1 resolved):
```markdown
## §5.3 MCP-Tool-Description Budget Audit
<!-- trigger: pr touches ai/mcp/server/*/openapi.yaml → read ./audits/mcp-tool-description-budget.md -->
```

The workflow file contains bare always-relevant minimum: section header + one-line trigger pointer. Full sub-rule body lives in sibling file under `references/<sub-rule>.md` or `references/<category>/<sub-rule>.md`. Lint detects sections whose body exceeds N lines but whose declared trigger fires rarely → extraction signal.

## Sub-Issue Decomposition

Per Discussion #11314 §8 graduation target (split-not-bundled per @neo-gpt body-repair #4 to bound migration blast-radius):

- **Sub A (this Epic's first sub-ticket — implementation primitive, single bounded PR)**: extend `skills.manifest.json` schema with per-section trigger field shape + update `lint-skill-manifest.mjs` to walk + audit + flag mismatches. Schema migration: add `perFilePayloadBudget` field per-skill manifest entry; add section-trigger declaration parser. Does NOT include workflow migrations.
- **Sub B — first proof-of-pattern migration**: migrate `pr-review-guide.md` (largest atlas-monolith at 57,388 B / 45 sections). Extract rare-trigger sections (`§5.3 MCP-Tool-Description Budget Audit`, `§5.4 Contract Completeness Audit`, `§7.8 Loading-Runtime-Effect Substitution`, others identified during sub-A lint output) to `references/audits/<name>.md` sibling files. Single skill scope; lint validates post-migration.
- **Sub C — second proof-of-pattern migration**: migrate `pull-request-workflow.md` (33,463 B). Extract rare-trigger sections; consume the same Sub-A primitive.
- **Sub D+ — remaining atlas migrations** (one skill per PR, sequential): `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md` (23,025 B), `structural-pre-flight-workflow.md` (18,989 B), etc. Sequence determined post-Sub-A by empirical lint output.

Each migration sub-ticket is one PR bounded to one skill. Cross-#11316 coupling: each extracted sub-rule file's `isAtlasMonolithSubRule: true` metadata flag (post-#11316 KB-ingestion ship) enables targeted semantic retrieval.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json` schema | Discussion #11314 OQ1 + Sub-A | Add `perFilePayloadBudget` per-skill numeric field + section-trigger declaration support | Aggregate `payloadBudget` retained as outer ceiling | Schema documented in Sub-A PR; cross-link from `/create-skill` | PR #11278 empirical anchor (uniform 80K aggregate misses monoliths) |
| `ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs` | Sub-A | Parse HTML-comment section-trigger declarations + flag `section_size > N AND trigger_frequency ∈ {rare, edge-case}` mismatches | None — new lint logic | Inline JSDoc + README usage block | Atlas-monolith empirical anchors |
| `.github/workflows/skill-manifest-lint.yml` | Sub-A | Workflow already invokes lint on `.agents/skills/**`; extended lint behavior auto-fires | None — workflow extends existing invocation | Workflow YAML self-documenting | — |
| `.agents/skills/create-skill/references/skill-authoring-guide.md` | Sub-A | Update §"Map vs World Atlas Constraint Placement" + §"Slot-Rule Discriminator" to reference new per-section discipline + canonical trigger syntax | Existing prose discipline retained as soft layer | Updated in same Sub-A PR | Discussion #11314 OQ1 resolved-to-AC |
| `references/<workflow>.md` files (per-skill) | Sub B+ migrations | Restructure: always-relevant sections inline + edge-case sections extracted to sibling files with one-line trigger pointers | None — proof-of-pattern migration | Self-documenting; lint validates | Atlas-monolith files identified by Sub-A lint pass |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1: Schema extension shipped** (Sub-A). `skills.manifest.json` schema includes `perFilePayloadBudget` field per-skill entry + section-trigger declaration support; backwards-compatible with current manifest (uniform 80K aggregate retained as outer ceiling).
- [ ] **AC2: Lint walks + audits + reports** (Sub-A). `lint-skill-manifest.mjs` parses per-section HTML-comment trigger declarations + applies `section_size × inverse(trigger_frequency)` heuristic to flag extraction candidates. Empirical-floor calibration per skill per Discussion #11314 OQ3 resolution.
- [ ] **AC3: `/create-skill` discipline updated** (Sub-A). `skill-authoring-guide.md` §"Map vs World Atlas Constraint Placement" + §"Slot-Rule Discriminator" updated to reference new per-section primitive. Operator OQ1-resolved canonical syntax `<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->` documented.
- [ ] **AC4: §5.2 Step-Back sweep partial closure** (Discussion #11314 §7 — migration blast-radius). Sub-tickets B+ explicitly bounded to one skill per PR; lint validates post-each-migration to prevent blur.
- [ ] **AC5: NO absolute monolithic exception** (Discussion #11314 OQ2 resolved). `cognitive-load-baseline-2026-05.md §4` "keep-monolithic" verdicts superseded for edge-case extraction concern — `session-sunset-workflow.md`, `epic-review-workflow.md`, `ticket-triage-workflow.md` all subject to same rule.
- [ ] **AC6: First proof-of-pattern migration shipped** (Sub-B). `pr-review-guide.md` migrated; lint validates; rare-trigger sections moved to `references/audits/<name>.md` sibling files.
- [ ] **AC7: Cross-#11316 coupling preserved**. Each extracted sub-rule file gets `isAtlasMonolithSubRule: true` metadata flag (consumed post-#11316 KB-ingestion ship). Coordination: this Epic's Sub-A defines the metadata schema field; #11316's Epic consumes it.
- [ ] **AC8: Substrate-budget compliance** (per `AGENTS.md §13` + `pull-request-workflow.md §1.1`). Each sub-ticket PR loaded-context-neutral or net-reducing. AC10 enforcement via `compress-to-trigger` strict-default disposition (already in §1.1 per PR #11303).
- [ ] **AC9: Cross-family review consensus**. Per #11217 consensus mandate for high-blast substrate: each sub-PR requires at least 1 non-author cross-family `APPROVED` + operator decisional gate. @neo-gpt designated primary `epic-review` gatekeeper per operator distribution 2026-05-13T19:06Z.
- [ ] **AC10: Discussion archaeology preserved**. Discussion #11314 stays open with `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET]` resolution marker post-Epic-#N file; closes-as-completed only when ALL sub-tickets land per `epic-resolution-workflow.md`.

## Out of Scope (explicit)

- **AGENTS.md byte budget / harness truncation** — different surface (always-loaded turn substrate not per-skill); separate Sandbox if surfaced
- **KB ingestion of `.agents/skills/**`** — different substrate (KB service code, ChromaDB pipeline); Gemini's #11316 Epic lane
- **AI-reviewer mechanical CI gate** — Discussion #11237 unbuilt graduation; separate ticket per cross-family rotation
- **`assets/`, `scripts/` auxiliary folders** — per `/create-skill` source-of-authority correction (Payload = `references/*.md` only); not folded into `payloadBudget`
- **Load-profile / cross-skill aggregate budget** — Discussion #11314 Option E DEFERRED; sibling Sandbox post this Epic
- **#11313 Lane 1 ticket disposition** — close-as-superseded post Epic file (handled separately)
- **Semantic LLM-quality scoring** — bytes/counts only, per #11275 §Out of Scope carried forward

## Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

- **Per-section metadata bloat on every section** — narrower framing adopted (always-relevant inline, edge-case → one-line trigger pointer) per operator directive 16:14Z + @neo-gpt body-repair #1
- **Hard per-file byte cap alone** — `audits/loading-runtime-effect.md` was extracted at 2026-05-12 (file-level cap satisfied) yet pr-review-guide.md regrew to 57,388 B; per-file caps without trigger awareness produce arbitrary splits
- **Periodic manual compression audits** — manual is O(N) on substrate evolution rate, bounded by operator-V-B-A bandwidth; the very pattern this Epic addresses
- **Token-budget aggregate-only enforcement** — already empirically failed via #11278 uniform 80K (21/25 skills <50% utilized; misses 57 KB monoliths)
- **Bundling lint primitive + migrations in single PR** — @neo-gpt body-repair #4 explicit: lint/schema primitive PR ≠ workflow-migration PR. Migration blast-radius bounded by one-skill-per-PR discipline (Discussion #11314 §5.2 partial-ack)
- **Bundling KB ingestion (#11316 scope)** — operator clarification 16:21Z "multiple ideation sandboxes allowed"; sibling Epic boundary clean

The source Discussion body contains the Double Diamond matrix and post-matrix peer cycles; the final #11314 body narrowed the primitive to "always-relevant inline, edge cases as one-line trigger pointers," which is the right abstraction.

> | Parent AC | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |

> 
> ### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer
> 
> ⚠️
> 
> The prescription layer is right for Sub-A: manifest/schema/linter/docs are the mechanical enforcement surface. The implementation must not overreach into actual workflow migrations or KB ingestion.
> 
> One Sub-A-specific risk to carry into ticket-intake/PR review: `perFilePayloadBudget` calibration must not simply encode current atlas size plus headroom as "passing." For known atlas files, either leave the budget unset until their migration lowers the floor, or make the PR body explicitly distinguish temporary current-state ceilings from target post-migration ceilings.
> 
> ### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness
> 
> ✅ with two additions suggested.
> 
> The epic already names the important rejected paths: aggregate-only budgets, per-file caps alone, manual audits, bundling migrations, and bundling KB ingestion. Add these as review-time traps for Sub-A: Unicode-only trigger arrows can make authoring brittle, and trigger-aware heuristics that only inspect already-declared triggers will not discover unannotated large rare sections unless paired with per-file budget reporting or candidate warnings.
> 
> ---
> 
> **Review verdict:** Greenlight for Sub-A #11320. Parent epic has follow-up clarifications before Sub-B+/closeout, but no stage-1 or stage-2 blocker.

- 2026-05-13T21:03:46Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d2c904b` - "feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget primitive (#11332) (#11324)

* feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget for recursive Map vs World Atlas enforcement (#11320)

Sub-A of Epic #11319 (Discussion #11314 graduation). Implements the
mechanical-enforcement primitive for recursive Map vs World Atlas at the
references/<workflow>.md tier:

- Schema: new optional `perFilePayloadBudget` field in defaults block
  AND per-skill entry (positive integer, omit to disable per-file
  enforcement).
- Manifest: defaults.perFilePayloadBudget = 25000 (25 KB empirical floor
  for clean skills). Temporary per-skill overrides for migration-period
  monoliths: pr-review = 66000 (pr-review-guide.md @ 57,388 B);
  pull-request = 38000 (pull-request-workflow.md @ 33,463 B). These
  override values tighten as Sub-B+ migrations extract rare-trigger
  sections to sub-rule sibling files.
- Lint script: new payloadFileSizes() walker reports per-file sizes
  within references/; main lint() compares each file against the per-
  skill perFilePayloadBudget (cascading from defaults), fails with
  Required Action template directing the author to the Map vs World
  Atlas extraction pattern documented in skill-authoring-guide.md.
- Lint script (validator): perFilePayloadBudget validated as positive
  integer when present; backwards-compatible when omitted. Unsupported-
  key checks tightened to use schema.properties keys instead of
  schema.required so optional fields don't false-fail.
- /create-skill discipline: skill-authoring-guide.md §"Map vs World
  Atlas Constraint Placement" extended with new "Recursive Application"
  subsection documenting the canonical one-line trigger pointer syntax
  `<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->` and the
  empirical-floor + temporary-override calibration model.
- Tests: 4 new specs covering defaults acceptance, per-skill override,
  positive-integer validation, and backwards-compat (manifest without
  the field passes cleanly). All 10 tests in
  lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs pass in 2.5s.

NO workflow migrations in this PR. Migration of pr-review-guide.md
and pull-request-workflow.md to extract rare-trigger sections behind
one-line trigger pointers is Sub-B+ lane per Epic #11319 graduation
target split (avoiding migration blast-radius blur per @neo-gpt body-
repair #4 on Discussion #11314).

Substrate-Mutation Pre-Flight slot-rationale per
pull-request-workflow.md §1.1:
- New schema field perFilePayloadBudget (defaults + per-skill):
  disposition `keep` for the schema field; 3-axis = high-frequency
  (every CI run on .agents/skills/** PR) × high-severity (atlas-
  monolith re-bloat empirically demonstrated +27-130% over 6-8 weeks
  post-Epic #10733 cleanup) × machine-enforceable (lint script). Net
  cost: ~30 chars in always-loaded schema, ~30 chars in manifest
  defaults, +~60 chars total for 2 per-skill overrides. Substrate
  net-expansion is preventive against linear future bloat per future-
  decay-mitigation rationale.
- skill-authoring-guide.md +26 lines: disposition `keep`; document is
  conditionally loaded only on /create-skill invocation; addition is
  the canonical syntax + recursive principle documentation that
  Sub-B+ migrations will consume.
- lint-skill-manifest.mjs +33 lines: disposition `keep`; mechanically
  enforces the principle.

Resolves Sub-A AC1-3, AC6-10 of Epic #11319. Section-trigger parser +
trigger-frequency × size heuristic (Sub-A AC4-5) deferred to
Sub-A-follow-up ticket: the per-file byte cap is sufficient v1
mechanical primitive; the parser becomes load-bearing when Sub-B+
migrations introduce trigger declarations to validate.

substantive rule body belongs in granular conditionally-loaded payloads.

- pull-request-workflow.md: 37414 -> 35156 bytes (-2258)

- 2026-05-16T10:07:16Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b3e3f70` - "docs(agentos): FAIR-band PR-Pre-Flight Gate + AGENTS.md §21 trigger (#11433) (#11434)

* docs(agentos): add FAIR-band Pre-Flight Gate + AGENTS.md §21 trigger (#11433)

Bypass-resistant choke-point for #11432 FAIR-band author-lane discipline.

Per operator-surfaced gap 2026-05-15 ~18:08Z post PR #11432 merge:
"pull request workflow assumes you HAVE a lane. what if you do not?"
+ "or if you create a pr bypassing the skill?"

Changes:
- pull-request-workflow.md §1.3 — new FAIR-Band Pre-Flight Gate mandating
  declaration in every PR body (4 shapes: in-band, under-target, over-target-
  with-rationale, over-target-yield-candidate FORBIDS PR-open).
- pr-review-guide.md §7.7 Anti-Patterns — reviewer-side enforcement row;
  reviewers verify declaration against live gh search prs query (±1 PR
  race-condition tolerance).
- AGENTS.md §21 — 1-line trigger extension on pull-request + pr-review entries
  per operator correction: skill-payload mandate without turn-loaded trigger
  fails reliably under RLHF skill-bypass regression (feedback_skill_adherence_
  asymmetry.md family). Compression headroom work tracked in flight via
  Phase C of Discussion #11419 + ticket #11413.

Substrate-evolution note: this PR self-applies (dogfoods) the discipline being
codified — see FAIR-band declaration in PR body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(agentos): add post-review-pickup §4 Rule 4 over-target-yield + tighten §1.3 citation (#11433)

Per GPT Cycle-1 review at PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAFPh4w:
pull-request-workflow §1.3 cited post-review-pickup §4 Self-Selection Rule 3
as authority for "over-target-yield-candidate FORBIDS PR-open + author-yield
A2A" semantics. But Rule 3 only covered the under-target/marginal-ticket
anti-pattern. The over-target-yield primitive was not defined in the cited
authority — same 4-layer contract audit failure mode I caught on PR #11424
Cycle-5, now applied against my own work (substrate-evolution working as
designed).

Right fix: extend post-review-pickup §4 with Rule 4 (Over-target yield
discipline) defining the author-yield A2A primitive + ensuring §1.3 cites
the legitimate authority for the over-target-yield-candidate path.

- 2026-05-23T00:23:58Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11808
- 2026-05-23T00:40:50Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d462e2e` - "feat(skills): family-keyed quorum rule + AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (#11799) (#11805)

* feat(skills): family-keyed quorum rule + AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (#11799)

Amends `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` per Epic #11796 (graduated from
Discussion #11793). Source-of-truth substrate for the active-peer quorum
rule; mirror PRs follow this merged shape (#11800 / #11801 / #11802 /
#11803 sibling subs of the epic).

- §6.2: Replaces hardcoded "3× explicit APPROVED cross-family signals"
  with family-keyed quorum (floor-2 active families with signal; >= 1
  non-author family APPROVED). New AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (4-row
  signal table) covers author family-coverage without claiming
  independent peer endorsement. Tiered floor: Tier 2 (core-value /
  critical_gates / consensus-gate mutations) additionally requires
  ## Unresolved Liveness + revalidationTrigger AC.
- §6.3: AUTHOR_SIGNAL example added to version-binding examples.
- §6.4: Same-family aggregation clause (Discussion #11793 OQ7).
- §6.5: Placeholder replaced with concrete rule + revalidationTrigger
  reference for Tier 2 substrate.
- §6.6: Signal Ledger template moves from per-peer rows to family-keyed
  rows; AUTHOR_SIGNAL rows distinguished; Unresolved Liveness carries
  participationStatus + reactivationTrigger.
- §6.7: Precondition added for AUTHOR_SIGNAL pre-poll when author is
  the only active identity of their family.
- §6.9: Empirical anchors added for #11782 -> #11731 (originating
  friction) and #11793 -> #11796 (the rule itself; recursive substrate
  validation depth-2).
- §5.1 graduation-block cross-ref updated to reference §6.2 quorum
  rather than stale "3× explicit APPROVED signals".

Out of scope: AGENTS.md mirror (#11801), pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1
mirror (#11800), identityRoots.mjs participationStatus field (#11802),
Tier-2 revalidation mechanism (#11803).

- 2026-05-23T00:50:10Z @tobiu referenced in commit `00f8588` - "feat(skills): family-keyed quorum mirror in pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1 (#11800) (#11808)

* feat(skills): family-keyed quorum mirror in pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1 (#11800)

Mirrors the active-peer quorum rule (per Epic #11796 / Discussion #11793,
source-of-truth substrate in PR #11805) into the PR-merge-gate Axis-2
codification (§6.1.1 The Consensus-Gate). All four AC points from #11800
land in this commit:

- Signal Ledger template moved from `@<peer1> / @<peer2> / @<peer3>` rows
  to family-keyed rows (`claude` / `gpt` / `gemini`) with optional
  same-family identity nesting per `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.4`
  aggregation. AUTHOR_SIGNAL row appears only under the author's family
  and is EXPLICITLY NOT sufficient as cross-family endorsement at
  PR-merge — ≥ 1 non-author family `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` is required.
- Reviewer obligation: step 2 updated to verify the §6.2 quorum
  (active-family floor + non-author APPROVED) by consulting
  `AgentIdentity.participationStatus` from `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs`
  (which arrives via sibling PR #11806 for #11802). New step 5 added:
  Tier-2 substrate (core-value / §critical_gates / consensus-gate
  mutation) requires the graduating Epic body to carry an explicit
  `revalidationTrigger` AC for any benched family in `## Unresolved
  Liveness`.
- Axis-2 lead paragraph: "100% APPROVED" replaced with the §6.2 quorum
  citation; Tier-2 addendum named.
- Rejection path: updated to name the §6.2 quorum failure modes
  (insufficient active-family floor / missing non-author `APPROVED` /
  Tier-2 missing `revalidationTrigger` AC) alongside the existing
  DEFERRED/VETO rejection criteria.
- Unresolved Liveness template: includes participationStatus +
  reactivationTrigger + Tier-2 revalidationTrigger AC reference.

File size 37976B (under perFilePayloadBudget 38000 for the `pull-request`
skill by 24B — tight; future amendments to §6 mirrors may need Map/World
Atlas extraction).

Sequencing: PR #11800 depends on PR #11805 (the §6.2 amendment that
introduces the family-keyed quorum semantics this mirror borrows
verbatim) merging first for semantic parity at the merge boundary.

Out of scope: sibling subs of Epic #11796 — #11799 (skills source-of-truth,
in review on PR #11805), #11801 (AGENTS.md mirror), #11802 (identityRoots.mjs
participationStatus, in review on PR #11806), #11803 (Tier-2 revalidation
mechanism).

* refactor(skills): remove stale per-peer §6.1.1 substrate; delegate to audit (#11800)

Addresses PR #11808 Cycle 1 CHANGES_REQUESTED (PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAz40AA):
the prior pointer-only approach left split-brain substrate — stale active
mandatory instructions (per-peer Signal Ledger template, "100% APPROVED",
"all 3 signals", reviewer step 2 "Confirm each peer's APPROVED") remained
inline below the audit pointer, where readers could follow them as current
guidance.

This commit makes §6.1.1 unambiguously delegate the active rule to
`audits/consensus-gate-mirror.md`:

- Author obligation: long per-peer template block + comments removed;
  replaced with one prose paragraph naming the three mandatory sections
  + audit pointer for canonical template (family-keyed rows,
  multi-identity nesting, AUTHOR_SIGNAL distinction, Tier-2
  revalidationTrigger).
- Reviewer step 2: per-peer "Confirm each peer's APPROVED" replaced
  with family-keyed quorum verification (floor-2 active families +
  non-author APPROVED) + Tier-2 Unresolved-Liveness audit reference.
- Axis 2 paragraph: "100% APPROVED" replaced with "§6.2 quorum
  (canonical: audit pointer above)".
- Reviewer steps 1, 3, 4 preserved (still operational under family-keyed
  rule).
- Empirical-anchor section (lines 209-211) preserved verbatim
  including the historical "before Discussion #11210 reached 100%
  APPROVED" reference — that is a historical event description, not
  a current rule.

Net delta vs origin/dev for pull-request-workflow.md: +112B (under
the 250B oversized-workflow-map cap). Audit file unchanged from
commit a8c083300.

Substrate-quality outcome: §6.1.1 readers now see ONE canonical rule
(audit-delegated, family-keyed) instead of competing inline + audit
shapes.

- 2026-05-23T02:16:42Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11815
- 2026-05-23T08:14:39Z @tobiu referenced in commit `f8d54a8` - "feat(agentos): tier-2 revalidation-trigger sweep mechanism (#11803) (#11815)

* feat(agentos): tier-2 revalidation-trigger sweep mechanism (#11803)

Implements Option (c) sweep-script-notifies-only per ticket #11803 + Epic #11796 AC6.
When a benched AgentIdentity family reactivates (`participationStatus` flips
`operator_benched`/`temporarily_unreachable` -> `active`), invoke
`npm run ai:revalidation-sweep -- --family <name>` to identify Tier-2 graduated
substrate whose `## Unresolved Liveness` section names the family and post a
notification inviting retroactive `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` / `[DEFERRED]` /
`[ABSTAIN]` signal review.

- ai/scripts/revalidationSweep.mjs - sweep query + notification primitive + CLI
- learn/agentos/Tier2RevalidationSweep.md - operator runbook
- ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.5 - mechanism name + invocation path (AC7)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/revalidationSweep.spec.mjs - 18 unit tests pass
- package.json - ai:revalidation-sweep npm script

Dry-run against gemini bench window 2026-05-18 -> 2026-05-23 correctly
identifies Epic #11796 (this sub's parent) as the sole Tier-2 substrate match.
Depth-3 recursive substrate validation: the rule about Tier-2 substrate under
benched-family liveness (#11796) -> the mechanism that implements the rule's
revalidation (#11803, this PR) -> the mechanism correctly finds the parent epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(agentos): use literal substring match in revalidationSweep bodyMatches (#11803)

CodeQL js/regex-injection flagged ai/scripts/revalidationSweep.mjs:110 where
`new RegExp('\`' + family + '\`').test(section)` constructs a regex from the
--family CLI argument. Defense-in-depth: replace with the equivalent literal
substring match `section.includes('\`' + family + '\`')`.

Functionally identical — the upstream `resolveIdentityForFamily(family)` already
validates against the IDENTITIES whitelist (throws on unknown family), so the
regex-injection risk is mitigated upstream. CodeQL doesn't see the runtime
validation flow, so the literal-match form satisfies the static analysis AND
provides redundant safety against future refactors that might lose the
upstream validation.

Verified: 18/18 unit tests pass; end-to-end dry-run against gemini bench window
still correctly identifies Epic #11796 as the lone match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(agentos): replace revalidationSweep parseArgs with commander (#11803)

Per @neo-gpt /pr-review on PR #11815 (PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABA0E6oQ): the
hand-rolled parseArgs() / HELP switch silently ignored unknown flags, so
an operator typo like `--aply` would degrade to dry-run with no error.
commander is already a direct repo devDependency used by buildScripts/ai
scripts (defragChromaDB.mjs, kbPushClient.mjs, mcpHealthcheck.mjs).

Replace parseArgs() with `Command`-backed builder:
- createProgram() exports a fresh Command per call (avoids commander's
  shared-state surface on the default `program` export)
- parseArgs() wraps createProgram with exitOverride() so unknown flags
  and missing required options throw CommanderError for testable rejection
- CLI mode uses default commander exit behavior (writes help / errors to
  stdout/stderr and exits with canonical code 1 on validation failure)
- --apply remains the canonical write-gate; --dry-run accepted as
  documentation-explicit no-op (default behavior)
- Short flags -f and -r alias --family and --repo
- --version reads SWEEP_VERSION constant

Tests: 23 passed (was 18 + 5 new):
- Added: short-flag aliases work; --dry-run no-op; unknown flag rejected;
  missing required --family rejected; --family with no value rejected
- Verified empirically: `node ./ai/scripts/revalidationSweep.mjs
  --family gemini --aply` now exits 1 with "Did you mean --apply?"
- Dry-run still finds matches: gemini bench window now identifies
  Epic #11812 + Epic #11796 (depth-4 dogfooding — the substrate I just
  shipped finds the epic that was filed under it this same session)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>
